Several updates:
Sessions: Change the default reading sessions per week from 3 to 4. Remove the "sessions per week" dropdown from the Add Book form entirely.

Articles: Add a "Done" toggle on each article. When toggled, the article moves under the "Complete" heading.
Book editing: Add an edit button on each book widget that lets the user update the book's settings (title, total pages, current page).
Primary/Secondary scheduling: Confirm that each week schedules sessions for both the Primary and Secondary book simultaneously. Primary should receive roughly 2/3 of the weekly reading pages, Secondary 1/3.

In the OutlineEditor and Brainstorm contenteditable editors, add two behaviors to Q chips:

Drag to reorder: Make Q chips draggable within the contenteditable div. On dragstart, store the chip's quote ID. On drop, remove the chip from its original position and insert it at the drop target position within the text.
Delete with confirm: When a Q chip is clicked and the popover opens, add a trash icon button in the popover. Clicking it shows a confirmation prompt ('Remove this link?') inline in the popover with Cancel and Confirm buttons. On confirm, remove the chip from the editor and update the serialized content in Supabase. The quote itself is NOT deleted from the Quotes tab — only the inline link is removed.

Apply both behaviors to both the Outline and Brainstorm editors. Write in parts if needed to stay under output limits. Push when done.

1.content:
In app/api/autofill-media/route.ts, update the autofill logic to actually fetch the URL's HTML before calling Claude. 

- Use fetch() to retrieve the page HTML (set a User-Agent header: 'Mozilla/5.0' to avoid bot blocks)
- Extract the following meta tags from the HTML: og:title, og:description, article:author, og:site_name, article:published_time, og:audio, and the plain <title> tag
- Pass this extracted metadata as context to Claude alongside the URL, instead of asking Claude to infer from the URL alone
- Claude should then map these to the appropriate fields: title, author, publication, date, show_title, guests
- If the fetch fails (network error, bot block, etc.), fall back to the existing inference-from-URL approach silently

display: [Pasted text #1 +10 lines]

1.content:
In app/api/autofill-media/route.ts, update the Claude prompt to also parse the URL slug itself for title and author clues. For a URL like:

https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/09/technology/anthropic-defense-artificial-intelligence-lawsuit.html

The slug "anthropic-defense-artificial-intelligence-lawsuit" contains enough information to construct a reasonable title. Update the system prompt to instruct Claude to:

1. Parse the URL slug (the final path segment before .html) and convert it from kebab-case to a properly capitalized title as a fallback when og:title metadata is unavailable
2. For NYT and similar outlets, do not return null for title — always derive a best-guess title from the slug
3. For author, if the fetch failed or returned no author metadata, return null rather than guessing — do not fabricate author names

Also add a console.log in the route that logs the raw HTML fetch result length and the first 500 characters, so we can debug whether the page fetch is succeeding or being blocked.

1.content:
In app/api/autofill-media/route.ts, update the response so that when a field cannot be retrieved, instead of returning null, return the string "Could not retrieve — please enter manually" for title and author fields only. publication, date, show_title, and guests should still return null when unavailable since those fields are optional and an unfilled optional field is less confusing than a placeholder.

Then in AddItemModal.tsx, update the autofill handler so that when it receives "Could not retrieve — please enter manually" as a value, it populates the field with that string but also:
- Styles the field with a subtle amber border (border-amber-300) to draw attention
- Selects all the text automatically (input.select()) so the user can immediately start typing to replace it
- Only applies this to the title and author fields

The folder pill on homepage cards (FolderPill component) and the Q chip buttons in the Outline/Brainstorm editors currently look too similar to each other. Differentiate them:

- Folder pill: change to a small navy background (bg-navy-800 text-white) with a tiny folder icon (16x16 SVG) before the name. Remove the amber border styling.
- Q chips in the editors: keep them amber (bg-amber-100 text-amber-800 border border-amber-300) but make them slightly more rectangular (rounded-md instead of rounded-full) with a small quotation mark character before the chip label so they read as quote references, not tags.

Do not change the folder picker dropdown or the Q chip popover behavior — just the visual appearance of the chips/pills themselves.

display: [Pasted text #2 +4 lines]

2.content:
In the Q chip popover (the tooltip/popover that appears when hovering a Q chip in the Outline or Brainstorm editor), fix the positioning so it never gets clipped at the bottom of the viewport.

Current behavior: the popover always renders below the chip, which cuts it off when the chip is near the bottom of the screen.

Fix: use getBoundingClientRect() on the chip element to check if there is enough space below it to render the popover. If window.innerHeight minus the chip's bottom position is less than the popover's estimated height (use 180px as a safe estimate), render the popover above the chip instead (bottom: 100% with a small offset). Otherwise render it below as normal. Do not constrain the popover height or add scroll — let it render at full height in whichever direction has more space.
